message:
fix(search): complete ENTER-overwrite and remque pointer-clear semantics

Two deterministic test failures surfaced by running the whole
frankenlibc-abi test suite in parallel locally (cargo test -p
frankenlibc-abi --tests) rather than just one binary at a time —
the local 64-core parallel run exposed them even though single-
binary runs passed. Both reproduce with --test-threads=1, so they
are not races.

## search_abi::HashTable::search on ENTER hit

Commit 65a8f6e3 ("refactor(abi): rustfmt pass + let-else cleanups
+ search.h ENTER fix") explicitly set out to implement the POSIX
hsearch(3) contract: "If action is ENTER and the item is already
present, the item's data value is set to the new value." The
commit message even says the fix overwrites data on hit. But the
code actually shipped with the OPPOSITE behavior and a comment
reading "POSIX/glibc return the existing entry for duplicate ENTER
calls without replacing its payload." The intended semantic fix
was never landed — the test hash_global_api (line 117) and
hash_reentrant_filled_tracks_unique_entries (line 619) fail
deterministically because the new data pointer is silently lost.

Fix: on ENTER hit, set slot.data = item.data before returning the
existing slot. This matches glibc hsearch(3), the POSIX text, and
65a8f6e3's original intent.

## search_abi::remque removed-element pointer-clear

remque(elem) unlinked elem from its doubly-linked list but left
elem->next and elem->prev pointing at the old neighbours. glibc
(and the standard Linux test expectation) clears both pointers to
NULL on the removed element, so a later remque of the same element
is a no-op rather than dereferencing stale neighbour pointers.

Fix: after the unlink, clear (*e).next and (*e).prev.

## Validation

cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test search_abi_test
  -> 33 passed, 0 failed (previously 30 passed, 3 failed on
  hash_reentrant_filled_tracks_unique_entries, hash_global_api,
  remque_middle).

Both failures reproduce with --test-threads=1 so they were pure
logic bugs, not races. Discovered by cc-libc during review-mode
parallel-abi-test stress run after bd-9hq64 was closed.

Agent: cc-libc
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
